decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip center. I will increase the spreader conductivity by a factor of 1.5 (from 425.25 to ~637.8), which is within the allowed 2.0x step limit and below the 500.0 upper bound constraint mentioned in the prompt text (note: prompt says bounds [20, 500] but current value is 425.25, so 500 is the hard cap. I must respect the 500 cap). Correction: The current value is 425.25. The max bound is 500.0. A 1.5x increase would be 637, which violates the bound. I will set it to the maximum allowed value of 500.0 to maximize heat spreading capability.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity to its upper bound maximizes lateral heat spreading, reducing the thermal gradient and lowering the peak chip temperature.

expected_effects: Reduced thermal resistance in the spreader layer
More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader
Chip peak temperature reduction towards or below 85.0°C
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions
Further improvements may require geometric changes (spreader width/height) if material limits are reached
